LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT AAA NORTHERN CALIFORNIA, NEVADA & UTAH INSURANCE'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS IT RELATES TO CLAIMS FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, EXEMPLARY DAMAGES, TREBLE DAMAGES, AND ATTORNEY'S FEES IN THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FINANCIAL ELDER ABUSE. BECAUSE DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND PARAGRAPHS 31, 33, 35 AND 37 ARE ALREADY STRICKEN FROM THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. CONSEQUENTLY, PARAGRAPH 7 OF PLAINTIFF'S PRAYER FOR RELIEF, SEEKING ATTORNEY FEES UNDER WELF. & INST. CODE 15657, IS ALSO STRICKEN. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE PARAGRAPH 50 FROM CAUSE OF ACTION FOUR, AND PARAGRAPHS 3 AND 6 IN THE PRAYER FOR RELIEF, IS GRANTED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFFS ARE GIVEN 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, IF THEY CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, THEY CAN PLEAD FACTS WITH SPECIFICITY THAT DEFENDANT'S OFFICER, DIRECTOR, OR MANAGING AGENT AUTHORIZED OR RATIFIED THE ALLEGED WRONGFUL CONDUCT. SEE CAL CIV. CODE 3294(B). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD KRAMER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

DEFENDANT AAA NORTHERN CALIFORNIA, NEVADA & UTAH INSURANCE INSURANCE EXCHANGE'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE ALLEGATION THAT DEFENDANT "TOOK, SECRETED, AND APPROPRIATED" PLAINTIFFS' DECEDENT'S PERSONAL PROPERTY FAILS AS A MATTER OF LAW. DEFENDANT DID NOT TAKE PLAINTIFFS' DECEDENT MONEY, AS SHE VOLUNTARILY ENTERED INTO A SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT WITH DEFENDANT FOR LESS THAN THE POLICY AMOUNT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. SCOTT RIGHTHAND, TEL. NO. 544-0115 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. CARRIE DUPIC HUYNH OF CODDINGTON, HICKS & DANFORTH, TEL. NO. (650) 592-5400 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT AAA NORTHERN CALIFORNIA, NEVADA & UTAH INSURANCE'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is granted. The motion is granted as it relates to claims for punitive damages, exemplary damages, treble damages, and attorney's fees in the second cause of action for financial elder abuse. Because Defendant's demurrer to the second cause of action is sustained without leave to amend paragraphs 31, 33, 35 and 37 are already stricken from the Second Amended Complaint. Consequently, paragraph 7 of Plaintiff's prayer for relief, seeking attorney fees under Welf. & Inst. Code 15657, is also stricken. Defendant's motion to strike paragraph 50 from cause of action four, and paragraphs 3 and 6 in the prayer for relief, is granted with 10 days leave to amend. Plaintiffs are given 10 days leave to amend, if they can do so in good faith, they can plead facts with specificity that Defendant's officer, director, or managing agent authorized or ratified the alleged wrongful conduct. See Cal Civ. Code 3294(b). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Richard Kramer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/RAK)
